title: Restore valid finalization receipts for ad-hoc Grabowski jobs
goal: Explain and repair why ad-hoc jobs launched through grabowski_job_start can finish with systemd success and complete logs while job status reports missing_finalization_evidence and finalization_receipt not_configured.

acceptance.assertion: The mismatch between configured ExecStopPost, metadata expectations and final status is reproduced and explained without trusting successful command logs alone.
A successful ad-hoc job emits a valid integrity-bound finalization receipt and status reports completed.
Launch, command and finalizer failures remain distinct and cannot be masked by postflight aggregation.
Tests cover short collected units, success, command failure, finalizer failure, missing unit and delayed status reads.

metadata.observation: Both jobs had systemd Result=success and complete successful logs, but status reported missing_finalization_evidence with finalization_receipt not_configured.
does_not_establish: that command logs alone are an integrity receipt
permission to alter running foreign jobs

root_cause.finding: Generic ad-hoc jobs configured ExecStopPost but did not persist a finalization contract; only runtime-deploy jobs had one. Successful command logs and systemd Result=success therefore could not establish a durable integrity-bound terminal result after unit collection.
root_cause.repair: Every newly accepted generic job now receives a hash-bound contract and a create-only finalization.json. Runtime deploy keeps its stronger expected-head, runner-owned receipt.
root_cause.truth_order: Visible systemd state remains primary. A persisted receipt is used only after the unit is collected or no longer visible and only after full contract, hash, path, identity and status validation.

validation.full_repository: make validate passed 1466 tests plus generated context, access policy, runtime locks, deploy tooling and secret scan

review.external_review_reason: The external read-only review sandbox could not read the frozen diff; no external verdict was represented as evidence.

failure_semantics.launch_failure: preserved from launch metadata and precedes receipt fallback
failure_semantics.command_failure: stored as failed with the terminal failure type
failure_semantics.finalizer_failure: visible as postflight failure and cannot convert a failed or successful main process into another main outcome

residual_risks: Same-UID code controlling both metadata and the systemd precondition can construct a self-consistent forgery; eliminating this requires a separate security domain.
Generic and runtime-deploy validators remain intentionally separate to avoid weakening expected-head authority.
Future runtime integrity is not established by this one deployment and live smoke.

metadata.verification_note: Grabowski PR #192 was reviewed against the exact GitHub diff, merged with green Python 3.10/3.12 CI, deployed at merge commit ed113109ec6693919071be832647b539a82c692d with a valid runner receipt, and verified by a real generic ad-hoc job whose valid create-only receipt restored succeeded after systemd unit collection.
